title: nasturtium cumingianum
intro: Nasturtium cumingianum, also known as Indian cress, is a tropical annual plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, derived from its leaves, flowers, and seeds. The leaves and flowers of Nasturtium cumingianum are rich in vitamins A and C, potassium, and other essential nutrients, offering several health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. Nasturtium cumingianum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, to treat various health conditions, and is also used as a natural food ingredient to add flavor and nutrition to dishes.
